text: Here we present a case of hand-assisted laparoscopic surgery for rectal cancer.
Fifty-eight female patient underwent screening colonoscopy.
A four-centimeter mass is identified fifteen centimeters from the anal verge during the rectal examination.
Biopsy showed moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma.
CT scan showed no distant metastasis.
The surgeon performs a six-centimeter transumbilical incision, creating an entry point for hand-assisted laparoscopic surgery.
The surgeon inserts a hand port through the transumbilical incision, providing access to the abdominal cavity for manual manipulation during the procedure.
Three working trocars are inserted into the abdominal cavity, providing access for surgical instruments.
The surgeon detaches adhesions from the spleen and immobilizes the splenic flexure, freeing these structures from surrounding tissue.
The peritoneum is visible on the medial side of the surgical field.
The inferior mesenteric artery (IMA) is mobilized and clipped at its origin, securing the vessel during the procedure.
The surgeon mobilizes the inferior mesenteric vein (IMV) and clips it just below the pancreatic border, ensuring clear separation from surrounding tissues.
The surgeon mobilizes the posterior side of the mesorectum, separating it from surrounding tissues.
The surgeon dissects the anterior side of the mesorectum, carefully separating it from surrounding tissues.
The surgeon transects the rectum using a linear stapler, completing the division of the rectal tissue.
The surgeon removes the bowel through a hand port, completing the extraction process following previous mobilization and clipping steps.
The surgeon performs an anastomosis using a circular stapler, joining the bowel ends following the resection of the rectum.
The surgeon performs a leak test on the anastomosis site, confirming the absence of leaks.
The surgeon closes the surgical wounds, completing the procedure.
The surgical specimen is displayed, showing the excised section of the rectum following the procedure.
Thank you.
